questionHtml: State one reason why monetary policy is considered to have limited effectiveness in increasing aggregate demand if an economy is in a deep recession.
markschemeHtml:
Answers may include:

  • Interest rates are already close to zero.
  • Spending depends on confidence/indebtedness as well as interest rates.
  • Money demand may be highly sensitive (elastic/flat) with respect to interest rates.
  • Investment/consumption may not be sensitive to changes in interest rates.

Any other valid reason.

Award [1] for one possible reason.

meta.title: State one reason why monetary policy is considered to have limited effectiveness in increasing aggregate demand if an economy is in a deep recession.
